docs.title: Local LLM Demo
docs.text:
A modular Python library + FastAPI service for interacting with local LLM backends (e.g. Ollama).

This project provides:

  • A reusable LocalLLM Python client
  • Streaming + non-streaming generation
  • Model lifecycle management
  • In-memory persistent sessions
  • A production-ready FastAPI HTTP API
  • Docker + Docker Compose setup

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Client (Python / curl / frontend) ↓ FastAPI (llm_local.api) ↓ LocalLLM ↓ OllamaHTTPClient ↓ Ollama backend ↓ Local model

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Docker Quickstart
  • Local Development
  • Streaming (SSE)
  • Sessions
  • Model Management
  • Python API Reference
  • HTTP API Reference

docs.title: Start everything
docs.text:
docker compose up --build
````

This starts:

* Ollama backend
* Automatic model pull
* FastAPI service

API runs at:

[http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000)

---

## Test the API

Health check:

```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/health

Generate text:

curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/generate \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"prompt": "Explain neural networks"}'

docs.title: List Models
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8000/models
````

---

## Pull Model

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/models/pull \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "llama3.2:3b"}'

docs.title: Persistent Sessions
docs.text:
Sessions are stored in-memory.

⚠ Not durable. Intended for dev/single-instance use.

docs.title: Create Session
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/sessions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"system_prompt": "You are helpful."}'
````

---

## Send Message

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/sessions/<id>/messages \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"message": "Hello!"}'

docs.title: LocalLLM
docs.text: Main client class for interacting with a local backend.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
from llm_local import LocalLLM

llm = LocalLLM()

text = llm.generate("Explain transformers.")
print(text)

docs.title: Streaming
docs.text:
for chunk in llm.generate_stream("Write a poem"):
    print(chunk, end="")

docs.title: Chat
docs.text:
messages = [
    {"role": "system", "content": "You are concise."},
    {"role": "user", "content": "Explain RL."}
]

reply = llm.chat(messages)
print(reply)
